articles.title: Why scrap is becoming central to the aluminium industry: the past fortnight's updates
articles.summary: A fortnightly roundup documents the accelerating global competition for aluminium scrap, noting that China — the world's largest primary producer — increased its overseas scrap imports in Q1 2026 even as its domestic recycling sector matures, as the industry's competitive advantage shifts from smelter scale to secondary material access and low-carbon processing.
articles.novelis_angle: Novelis's pre-secured scrap streams — JLR REALCAR closed-loop, the renewed Infinitum Norway collection agreement, and Latchford's UK DRS readiness — insulate it from the open-market scrap competition that is now intensifying globally.

articles.title: EU delays aluminium scrap export curbs till September: will India seize the opportunity?
articles.summary: The European Union has postponed implementation of its aluminium scrap export restriction measures until September 2026, pushing back from the original spring timeline. India — which sources approximately 80% of its domestic scrap demand through imports and was the largest EU scrap importer throughout 2025 and Q1 2026 — is evaluating whether to accelerate procurement before the curbs take effect.
articles.novelis_angle: The three-month delay extends current trade flows; once September restrictions take effect, EU-domiciled recyclers benefit from a tighter domestic scrap market that reduces feedstock competition from non-EU secondary producers — strengthening the economics of Novelis's high-recycled-content European mills.

articles.title: China's aluminium scrap sector squeezed by tax crackdown, shrinking aluminium scrap margins
articles.summary: China's 2026 VAT Law enforcement has suspended 'reverse invoicing' practices in multiple provinces, narrowing the refined-to-scrap spread to RMB 412/t as of 23 June and compressing secondary aluminium producers' margins; reduced scrap availability is tightening China's domestic recycling sector just as global scrap demand from Europe and North America intensifies.
articles.novelis_angle: Chinese secondary producers competing harder for international scrap to offset domestic supply constraints could push global scrap premiums higher, adding cost pressure for European recyclers with less-secured feedstock than Novelis.
